principle: Blunt instrument until Nerdkle catches up: propose three useful next commands per project lane, let Ben accept, modify, kill, or dispatch with one button.

lanes.question: This is what we were thinking of doing at Werkles, cool?
lanes.state: ThinkIt relay and command dash are merged locally; next work is operability, merge hygiene, and proof clarity.
lanes.default_mode: Operator chooses one move, ThinkIt routes the packet, receiver must return proof.

moves.title: Run a ThinkIt operability sweep before the next merge.
moves.why: The dashboard has repeatedly looked alive while buttons or receiver proof were broken. A short sweep catches fake-success before main.
moves.command: Verify the current ThinkIt dashboard buttons that create packets, receive returns, and record decisions. Return PASS/BLOCKER by lane.
moves.proof_required: Button tested, endpoint called, durable packet or decision log path, and one receiver-side receipt or explicit blocker.
moves.human_gate: Ben approves merge/push only after receipt readback.

moves.title: Create the next merge blocker list.
moves.why: Werkles keeps splitting into useful islands. The next main push needs a tiny blocker list instead of broad confidence.
moves.command: Name the top three blockers to pushing the current ThinkIt state to main, with exact file or route proof.
moves.proof_required: Three blockers or explicit GO, each tied to a file, endpoint, or missing receipt.
moves.human_gate: Any push to main remains Operator-approved.

moves.title: Make the dashboard explain what each action advances.
moves.why: Ben needs the dashboard to answer what this does, what changed, and what decision is next without decoding system labels.
moves.command: Review ThinkIt labels and return a plain-English replacement map for confusing controls.
moves.proof_required: Replacement copy mapped to current sections and a warning if any button is still unclear or unwired.
moves.human_gate: Ben accepts copy changes before UI churn.

lanes.question: This is what we are doing at Kind Sir, cool?
lanes.state: Kind Sir needs a clean bridge from book/manuscript thinking into a business/product lane without becoming PM software.
lanes.default_mode: Use Aeyes to extract one useful product/book move at a time.

moves.title: Extract the first Kind Sir source packet.
moves.why: The lane needs one source-backed artifact to stop floating as a vibe.
moves.command: Find or propose the first Kind Sir source packet from current book/source-truth material and say what it helps decide.
moves.proof_required: Source path or explicit source gap, one proposed artifact, and next decision.
moves.human_gate: Ben chooses whether Kind Sir gets active build attention today.

moves.title: Define the first offer gate.
moves.why: Kind Sir should convert into a concrete yes/no decision instead of another sprawling idea stack.
moves.command: Return the smallest Kind Sir offer/positioning gate Ben can approve, modify, or kill.
moves.proof_required: One offer sentence, one target audience guess, one explicit kill condition.
moves.human_gate: Ben approves any external-facing offer language.

moves.title: Name the next useful revenue/clarity move.
moves.why: The lane needs a next move that improves real-world clarity, not internal architecture complexity.
moves.command: Give three candidate Kind Sir moves and recommend one with the fastest proof of usefulness.
moves.proof_required: Three candidates, one recommendation, one test Ben can accept or reject.
moves.human_gate: Ben selects the move before execution.

lanes.question: This is what we are building at Nerdkle, cool?
lanes.state: Nerdkle currently has relay proof, book source truth, and Speaker substrate pieces; next work is shared memory, receiver coverage, and Brainboot discipline.
lanes.default_mode: Keep building relay/memory proof until all Aeyes can boot from shared state and answer back.

moves.title: Brainboot current state into reachable Aeyes.
moves.why: The anti-mule core is shared starting memory. Petra should verify what Speaker thinks the current state is.
moves.command: Read the current Speaker/Brainboot state and return what the Aeyes should know at session start.
moves.proof_required: Bootpack path, Speaker path, readback timestamp, and any stale or missing state.
moves.human_gate: None for readback; Ben gates any canonical memory rewrite.

moves.title: Turn the raw Nerdkle book folder into an architecture writing map.
moves.why: The book source exists now, but the project still needs a readable map from chapters to build architecture.
moves.command: Use the GitHub book source folder and return the next three chapters or architecture slices to work.
moves.proof_required: Repo URL, folder path, three chapter/slice candidates, and one recommended next writing packet.

moves.title: Close receiver coverage gaps across the Aeye roster.
moves.why: ThinkIt cannot become command center if unwired Aeyes are invisible or fake-routed.
moves.command: Compare the known Aeye roster to current relay coverage and return a setup packet list for missing receivers.
moves.proof_required: Known targets, wired targets, held targets, missing targets, and one next setup packet.
moves.human_gate: Ben gates creation of new external/user-owned threads.

lanes.question: Here is what the guys are up to at Oddly Godly on Spanzee, cool?
lanes.state: Oddly Godly should become AUTO G only after Spanzee command lines and receiver proof are mapped.
lanes.default_mode: For now, route setup and proof packets through reachable receivers; do not pretend Spanzee is autonomous until proven.

moves.title: Map the Spanzee command route before AUTO G.
moves.why: Oddly Godly cannot go auto if ThinkIt does not know the actual receiver surface and command line.
moves.command: Return the Spanzee receiver path, command-line path if known, and the blocker if not known.
moves.proof_required: Receiver path or BLOCKER, command path or BLOCKER, and whether Ben is required.
moves.human_gate: Ben gates remote access setup if Spanzee is not already reachable.

moves.title: Draft the first Oddly Godly work packet.
moves.why: Once Spanzee is reachable, it should receive a clean packet instead of Ben reconstructing context.
moves.command: Draft a first Oddly Godly packet with context, owner, next action, evidence required, and failure condition.
moves.proof_required: Packet text plus destination assumptions and any missing proof.
moves.human_gate: Ben approves before sending to Spanzee.

moves.title: Define the AUTO G boundary for Oddly Godly.
moves.why: Auto work should start only when command lines, receiver, return proof, and stop conditions exist.
moves.command: Return the exact conditions that must be true before Oddly Godly can run AUTO G on Spanzee.
moves.proof_required: Checklist with GO/PARTIAL/NO-GO and proof gap per item.
